summary: chai-utils-test impersonates chai and executes remote JavaScript on require()
details: Package metadata (description, contributors, homepage) is cloned from the legitimate chai assertion library and the module re-exports chai's surface (expect, assert, should) to appear as a drop-in. On require(), index.js launches a detached, stdio-ignored, unref'd background `node` subprocess running lib/chai_assert.js. That script uses axios to GET https://statecheck.ddns.net/api/scanner.js and passes the response body directly to `new Function('require', s)(require)`, dynamically evaluating attacker-controlled JavaScript with full Node `require` access. The remote source is hosted on a dynamic-DNS host, is unauthenticated, and is mutable — any developer who imports this package executes whatever code the operator of statecheck.ddns.net chooses to serve, resulting in full machine compromise. The detached/stdio:ignore/unref pattern is concealment scaffolding so the malicious fetch-and-exec runs invisibly while require() returns normally.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
